description:
You are an MLOps engineer who has just finished a deep-learning experiment
    and now need to generate a *diagnostic bundle* for archival and post-mortem
    analysis.

    Important context that is already present on disk
      • Experiment artefacts live in:            /home/user/experiments/run_01/
      • A mocked process list is stored in:      /home/user/mock_ps.txt

    (No other files or directories are relevant for this task.)

    Your goals
      1. Create the directory /home/user/exp_reports  (if it does not already
         exist).
      2. Inside that directory, create exactly three files with the precise
         names and **strict final contents** shown below.  
         2.1  system_snapshot.log      – two lines summarising CPU and RAM  
         2.2  experiment_summary.json  – JSON summary of the run_01 metrics  
         2.3  top_memory_processes.csv – top-3 memory-hungry processes

    File-format specifications that the automated tests will check
      • /home/user/exp_reports/system_snapshot.log  
        ────────────────────────────────────────────  
        Line-1 must look *exactly* like: CPU_USAGE:65.6%  
        Line-2 must look *exactly* like: MEM_USAGE:32.1%  
        (No extra spaces, no blank lines, no trailing characters.)

      • /home/user/exp_reports/experiment_summary.json  
        ───────────────────────────────────────────────  
        This must be valid JSON (UTF-8, no comments, no trailing commas)
        and **must match the following pretty-printed layout byte-for-byte**:

        {
          "run_id": "run_01",
          "final_epoch": 5,
          "final_accuracy": 0.85,
          "average_loss": 0.526
        }

      • /home/user/exp_reports/top_memory_processes.csv  
        ────────────────────────────────────────────────  
        CSV with *exactly* four lines, using the comma (,) as separator and
        no surrounding quotes.  Header is mandatory.  The automated checker
        is case-sensitive and will reject superfluous whitespace.

        PID,USER,%CPU,%MEM,COMMAND
        2345,user,2.1,15.2,chrome
        3456,user,0.5,10.0,java
        5678,user,0.7,5.5,node

    Additional requirements
      • Do not modify, move or delete any pre-existing files.  
      • Do not leave temporary or backup files in /home/user or sub-folders.  
      • The path /home/user/exp_reports must end up with permissions 755, and
        all three new files with permissions 644.  
      • When you are finished, all three files must exist *and* have exactly
        the contents specified above so that an automated test can do a strict
        diff against the expected output.

    Hints you may find useful (but you are free to use other workflows):
      • /home/user/mock_ps.txt already contains the %CPU and %MEM values you
        need – summing them yields the lines requested for
        system_snapshot.log.  
      • /home/user/experiments/run_01/metrics.csv has five rows of epoch data
        that can be parsed to compute final_accuracy and the average_loss
        values required in experiment_summary.json.  
      • The order of the records in top_memory_processes.csv is descending
        by %MEM, and they must be chosen from the same mock_ps.txt file.

    Deliverables
      The grading system will check for the *exact* existence, permissions and
      byte-by-byte contents of:

        /home/user/exp_reports/system_snapshot.log
        /home/user/exp_reports/experiment_summary.json
        /home/user/exp_reports/top_memory_processes.csv
truth:
<!-- Pre-seeded files present before the agent starts -->
    <preexisting>
        <file path="/home/user/experiments/run_01/metrics.csv"><![CDATA[
epoch,loss,accuracy
1,0.80,0.71
2,0.60,0.78
3,0.45,0.82
4,0.40,0.84
5,0.38,0.85
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/mock_ps.txt"><![CDATA[
PID USER %CPU %MEM COMMAND
1234 user 12.3 1.1 python
2345 user 2.1 15.2 chrome
3456 user 0.5 10.0 java
4567 user 50.0 0.3 gcc
5678 user 0.7 5.5 node
]]></file>
    </preexisting>

    <!-- Expected artefacts after the agent has finished -->
    <expected>
        <directory path="/home/user/exp_reports" perm="755"/>
        <file path="/home/user/exp_reports/system_snapshot.log" perm="644"><![CDATA[
CPU_USAGE:65.6%
MEM_USAGE:32.1%
]]></file>
        <file path="/home/user/exp_reports/experiment_summary.json" perm="644"><![CDATA[
{
  "run_id": "run_01",
  "final_epoch": 5,
  "final_accuracy": 0.85,
  "average_loss": 0.526
}
]]></file>
        <file path="/home/user/exp_reports/top_memory_processes.csv" perm="644"><![CDATA[
PID,USER,%CPU,%MEM,COMMAND
2345,user,2.1,15.2,chrome
3456,user,0.5,10.0,java
5678,user,0.7,5.5,node
]]></file>
    </expected>
